message:
fcp-oauth: make token refresh update atomic and reject empty fields

OAuthTokens::update_from_response previously silently skipped assigning
access_token and token_type when the refresh response carried empty
values for either field, but unconditionally bumped expires_at,
issued_at, refresh_token, scope, and id_token from the same response.
A compromised or misbehaving authorization server could therefore push
an empty access_token alongside a fresh expires_in and leave the old
access_token in place with a freshly-extended TTL — a Frankenstein
token where is_expired() and needs_refresh() both keep returning false
for an access_token that will no longer authenticate against the
provider. The silent skip papered over the symptom instead of flagging
the malformed response.

Change the contract:

  - update_from_response now returns OAuthResult<()> and validates the
    response up front. An empty access_token or empty token_type causes
    an immediate InvalidTokenResponse error with no mutation of self,
    making the update atomic — either the whole response is applied or
    none of it is.
  - Callers in crates/fcp-oauth/tests/no_mock_integration.rs and the
    in-crate refresh tests updated to propagate the Result.
  - Added test_update_from_response_rejects_empty_access_token_without_
    mutating to pin the atomicity guarantee, and a sibling test for
    empty token_type.

Behavioral impact: refresh flows that previously tolerated partially
empty refresh responses now surface them as errors to the caller,
which is the correct signal to drop the tokens and trigger a full
reauthorization rather than silently extending the lifetime of stale
credentials.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
